Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve. Round to one decimal place.
Question: Landscaping Reed wants to have a square garden plot in his backyard. He has enough compost to cover an area of $$75$$ square feet. How long can a side of his garden be?

Question: Landscaping Vince wants to make a square patio in his yard. He has enough concrete to pave an area of $$130$$ square feet. How long can a side of his patio be?

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve. Round to one decimal place.
Question: Gravity An airplane dropped a flare from a height of $$1,024$$ feet above a lake. How many seconds did it take for the flare to reach the water?

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, solve. Round to one decimal place.
Question: Gravity A hang glider dropped his cell phone from a height of $$350$$ feet. How many seconds did it take for the cell phone to reach the ground?

Question: Gravity A construction worker dropped a hammer while building the Grand Canyon skywalk, $$4,000$$ feet above the Colorado River. How many seconds did it take for the hammer to reach the river?

Decorating Denise wants to install a square accent of designer tiles in her new shower. She can afford to buy $$625$$ square centimeters of the designer tiles. How long can a side of the accent be?

Decorating Morris wants to have a square mosaic inlaid in his new patio. His budget allows for $$2,025$$ tiles. Each tile is square with an area of one square inch. How long can a side of the mosaic be?
Why is there no real number equal to $$\sqrt{-64} ?$$
What is the difference between $$9^{2}$$ and $$\sqrt{9} ?$$
Answer: Answers will vary. 9 2 reads: “nine squared” and means nine times itself. The expression $$\sqrt{9}$$ reads: “the square root of nine” which gives us the number such that if it were multiplied by itself would give you the number inside of the square root.
